events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need produce JSON answer. Need reason. Scenario: wrong breaker type for panel (listing). Work rough-complete. Need decide fail because non-compliant. Risk? Wrong breaker type for panel listing can be safety issue: overcurrent device not listed for use in panel, may not fit properly, interrupt rating, coordination, void listing, fire hazard. Risk high or critical? In commercial project, wrong breaker type for panel listing is significant but maybe high. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete rough-complete maybe 70? Value not asked 0. Sound etc default. Workable empty. Findings: breaker installed is not listed/approved for the panel; violates NEC 110.3(B) and 240. Actions: remove/replace with manufacturer-specified breaker type, verify panel labeling and submittals, retest/inspect. Rationale. References: NEC 110.3(B), NEC 240.6, 240.83 maybe, manufacturer listing. Also 110.14(C) termination temp ratings maybe relevant? Wrong breaker type could affect termination temp? Not directly but mention if applicable. Need ensure JSON valid.

    "Re-inspect and document before closing out the rough."
  ],
  "rationale": "Overcurrent protective devices must be listed and installed in accordance with the panelboard manufacturer's instructions and NEC 240. A breaker that is the wrong type for the panel is not listed for that use, creating a fire and reliability hazard, so the work fails compliance at rough-complete.",
